cowardice_flags.avoided: Reframe says both framings are right and that's the actual dilemma
cowardice_flags.should_say: The dilemma is fake. Thailand and Indonesia prove you can get the factory, jobs, AND affordable EVs without the 80% export quota or RM200k floor — Malaysia's conditions exist solely to shield Proton/Geely and Perodua's politically connected vendor ecosystem from real competition.

cowardice_flags.avoided: Mature card calls Johari's conditions not irrational and offers vague time-bound transition
cowardice_flags.should_say: The conditions are textbook crony protectionism dressed as national interest. Proton has had 40 years of tariffs and subsidies and still can't compete; the policy is about preserving elite patronage networks that cross every party.

false_balance.evidence_says: ASEAN peers show zero-sum is false — Thailand/Indonesia got BYD factories without export quotas or price floors and still deliver cheap EVs to their B40; Malaysia's unique restrictions protect foreign-owned incumbents at consumer expense.

audience_check: Taxi driver in Ipoh, teacher in Kota Bharu, hawker in Penang, farmer in Sabah, factory worker in Johor would roll their eyes — it speaks AT them with elite trade-off language and never says plainly that their RM130k dream EV is blocked so connected bosses keep collecting.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Four decades of bumiputera auto protectionism has produced dependency not competitiveness — Proton still needs heavy shielding while average Malay B40 families pay 30-50% more for cars than Thais or Indonesians; policy enriches connected Malay elites and their Chinese/foreign partners far more than it lifts the community.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Analyst dodges it to avoid being called racist or anti-Malay.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Every party (BN, PH, PN) treats the auto vendor ecosystem as patronage ATM — 130+ Proton vendors and 11,500 Perodua jobs are jobs for the boys, not national assets; MITI conditions are negotiated with Geely-linked players already inside the tent.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Calling out cross-party cronyism kills the polite considered view tone.

hard_truths_missing.truth: This is not race vs race — it's B40 multi-racial consumers subsidising M40/M25 white-collar and politically-linked manufacturing jobs; the RM200k floor is a regressive tax on the poor so elites keep their Proton/Geely margins.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Racial lens is safer and more familiar in Malaysian discourse than admitting class warfare.

gender_truths_avoided.truth: Auto manufacturing job losses would hit men disproportionately while B40 women bear the daily cost of expensive transport and delayed EV adoption — analysis never separates the gendered pain.
gender_truths_avoided.why_uncomfortable: Would require admitting protectionism protects male-dominated unions and patronage networks more than families.

class_truths_avoided.truth: Policy explicitly prices out B40 (30+ million Malaysians) from the EV transition to protect M40 factory jobs and vendor profits; Thailand shows you can industrialise without making the bottom 40% pay the bill.
class_truths_avoided.why_uncomfortable: Shifts narrative from national interest to straight class extraction.

predictions.if_nothing_changes: 6 months: BYD delays or walks from Tanjung Malim; 2 years: Thailand and Indonesia dominate ASEAN EV supply chain, Malaysia imports premium CBU EVs or sticks with expensive Proton/Perodua hybrids, B40 transport costs stay high, green transition stalls.
predictions.who_can_fix: MITI technocrats + PM Anwar's reformist faction
predictions.why_they_wont: Geely/Proton lobby + bumiputera policy sacred cows + fear of Malay vote backlash outweigh long-term consumer benefit; political survival beats economic logic every time.

strongest_card.why: The political fact card lands the real gut-punch irony (Geely owns Proton yet MITI blocks another Chinese player for being too affordable) without hedging.

weakest_card.why: The mature card collapses into diplomatic pablum — not irrational... time-bound transition — exactly the cowardly non-answer it accuses others of giving.

overall_assessment: Classic Malaysian policy porn: lots of facts, regional comparisons, and both sides theater that flatters the reader into thinking they've seen nuance, while surgically excising race-based patronage, decades of Proton failure, and the blunt truth that the rakyat are being robbed to keep elite vendor networks fat. It informs nobody who actually pays for petrol and car instalments.
